generatedSentence: A lone jockey, nicknamed 'Viking' for his wild eyebrow, sat in his backpack-adorned pod, watching the evening news on his mobile phone, an emoticon of concern flickering across his face. A strange occurrence on the eastbound roadway had caused a shiver down his spine: a massive data upwarp, theorized to be radiation from an ancient, unearthed computer. He was picking through old video feeds, his fingers rapidly navigating the internals of the network, hoping to uncheck the mounting digital vandalism. He needed to apply his skills, or the majority of the digital world would be dominated. The computer's algorithm, encrypted with old Kanji symbols, seemed to afforce a downward spiral, a magnetic resonance pulling the wasteland of online data closer. He inched forward, feeling the impulsive irony of his situation; he had balked at the school’s initial offer but now, faced with this overwhelming threat, had to accept. The pickoff of data streams was relentless, a fairy tale of digital necromancy unfolding. His minimal efforts were underwhelming, and he knew he couldn't repackage this digital monster alone. The full moon, an auspicious event, illuminated the raceway's ceiling, as the computer, now a hulking enigma of magnets and grinder, began to howl, its rotation creating viscera-like blossoms of code. A new wave of jammers, like kickers from a rival paddock, descended, their presence effectively hijacking the system. He made a snap decision, throwing his pinkie finger in an audacious act to throttle the main power grid. The roar of digital cannons and subsequent attacks heralded the climax, as ultimatums flashed across his field of vision. He had to be sparing; one wrong move, and the entire economic system would be junked. The newcomers, a vegan commune of coders nicknamed 'Butternut Cabbage,' had been cajoled by his plea for help via Facebook. They had rejected the previous day's offers, thinking him an idiot, but his genuine plight, the sheer density of the situation, changed their minds. Happily, they helped him pick apart the corrupted code, delivering a decisive kick to the beast, ensuring a happy resolution, a delight to all, and leaving only an ornamental emblem of the once great volcano-like server.
panelSentences: A lone jockey, nicknamed 'Viking' for his wild eyebrow, sat in his backpack-adorned pod, watching the evening news on his mobile phone, an emoticon of concern flickering across his face. A strange occurrence on the eastbound roadway had caused a shiver down his spine: a massive data upwarp, theorized to be radiation from an ancient, unearthed computer. He was picking through old video feeds, his fingers rapidly navigating the internals of the network, hoping to uncheck the mounting digital vandalism. He needed to apply his skills, or the majority of the digital world would be dominated.

The computer's algorithm, encrypted with old Kanji symbols, seemed to afforce a downward spiral, a magnetic resonance pulling the wasteland of online data closer. He inched forward, feeling the impulsive irony of his situation; he had balked at the school’s initial offer but now, faced with this overwhelming threat, had to accept. The pickoff of data streams was relentless, a fairy tale of digital necromancy unfolding. His minimal efforts were underwhelming, and he knew he couldn't repackage this digital monster alone.

The full moon, an auspicious event, illuminated the raceway's ceiling, as the computer, now a hulking enigma of magnets and grinder, began to howl, its rotation creating viscera-like blossoms of code. A new wave of jammers, like kickers from a rival paddock, descended, their presence effectively hijacking the system. He made a snap decision, throwing his pinkie finger in an audacious act to throttle the main power grid. The roar of digital cannons and subsequent attacks heralded the climax, as ultimatums flashed across his field of vision.

He had to be sparing; one wrong move, and the entire economic system would be junked. The newcomers, a vegan commune of coders nicknamed 'Butternut Cabbage,' had been cajoled by his plea for help via Facebook. They had rejected the previous day's offers, thinking him an idiot, but his genuine plight, the sheer density of the situation, changed their minds. Happily, they helped him pick apart the corrupted code, delivering a decisive kick to the beast, ensuring a happy resolution, a delight to all, and leaving only an ornamental emblem of the once great volcano-like server.
rawPanelSentences: A lone jockey, nicknamed 'Viking' for his wild eyebrow, sat in his backpack-adorned pod, watching the evening news on his mobile phone, an emoticon of concern flickering across his face. A strange occurrence on the eastbound roadway had caused a shiver down his spine: a massive data upwarp, theorized to be radiation from an ancient, unearthed computer. He was picking through old video feeds, his fingers rapidly navigating the internals of the network, hoping to uncheck the mounting digital vandalism. He needed to apply his skills, or the majority of the digital world would be dominated.

The computer's algorithm, encrypted with old Kanji symbols, seemed to afforce a downward spiral, a magnetic resonance pulling the wasteland of online data closer. He inched forward, feeling the impulsive irony of his situation; he had balked at the school’s initial offer but now, faced with this overwhelming threat, had to accept. The pickoff of data streams was relentless, a fairy tale of digital necromancy unfolding. His minimal efforts were underwhelming, and he knew he couldn't repackage this digital monster alone.

The full moon, an auspicious event, illuminated the raceway's ceiling, as the computer, now a hulking enigma of magnets and grinder, began to howl, its rotation creating viscera-like blossoms of code. A new wave of jammers, like kickers from a rival paddock, descended, their presence effectively hijacking the system. He made a snap decision, throwing his pinkie finger in an audacious act to throttle the main power grid. The roar of digital cannons and subsequent attacks heralded the climax, as ultimatums flashed across his field of vision.

He had to be sparing; one wrong move, and the entire economic system would be junked. The newcomers, a vegan commune of coders nicknamed 'Butternut Cabbage,' had been cajoled by his plea for help via Facebook. They had rejected the previous day's offers, thinking him an idiot, but his genuine plight, the sheer density of the situation, changed their minds. Happily, they helped him pick apart the corrupted code, delivering a decisive kick to the beast, ensuring a happy resolution, a delight to all, and leaving only an ornamental emblem of the once great volcano-like server.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. Protagonist: a cybernetic jockey, wearing a polished chrome viking helmet and a glowing data backpack. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The jockey at a holographic control panel, fingers hovering over a glowing map of a digital wasteland, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The jockey looking up in alarm at a massive, swirling data vortex, pulling in digital debris, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The jockey, a determined expression on his face, activating a wrist-mounted device as digital cannons fire around him, close-up. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The jockey, smiling, shaking hands with a group of diverse coders in futuristic vegan attire, against a backdrop of a newly peaceful, glowing digital city, eye-level shot.

definitions.definition: One of the Scandinavian or other Northern European seafaring warriors that raided (and then settled) the British Isles and other parts of Europe in the 8th to the 11th centuries and, according to many historians, were the first Europeans to reach North America.
(by extension) A stock character common in the fantasy genre, namely a barbarian, generally equipped with an axe or sword and a helmet adorned with horns.
A Norseman (mediaeval Scandinavian).
(mildly) An ethnic Swede, Norwegian, Dane, Icelander or Faroe Islander.
A player on the Minnesota Vikings NFL team.
A person who does not eat, drink or otherwise consume any animal products

A person committed to avoiding products and practices that inherently involve animal use, including all foods containing animal products, and to abstaining from direct and intentional harm to animals as far as possible; an adherent to veganism.
definitions.example: Our vegan-friendly shaving brush is made with synthetic bristles instead of badger hair.

definitions.definition: (of a product or practice, especially food) Not containing animal products (meat, eggs, milk, leather, etc) or inherently involving animal use.
definitions.example: He eats a completely vegan diet.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Committed to avoiding any product or practice that inherently involves animal use.
definitions.example: She is not vegan as she eats eggs and wears leather.

definitions.definition: Relating to vegans or veganism.
definitions.example: She is interested in vegan philosophy.

definitions.definition: A flexible sleeve made of latex or other impermeable material such as sheepskin, worn over an erect penis during intercourse as a contraceptive or as a way to prevent the spread of STDs.
A person or device that cuts (in various senses).
definitions.example: a stone cutter; a die cutter

definitions.definition: A single-masted, fore-and-aft rigged, sailing vessel with at least two headsails, and a mast set further aft than that of a sloop.
A foretooth; an incisor.
A heavy-duty motor boat for official use.
A ship's boat, used for transport ship-to-ship or ship-to-shore.
A ball that moves sideways in the air, or off the pitch, because it has been cut.
A cut fastball.
A ten-pence piece. So named because it is the coin most often sharpened by prison inmates to use as a weapon.
A person who practices self-injury.
A surgeon.
An animal yielding inferior meat, with little or no external fat and marbling.
An officer in the exchequer who notes by cutting on the tallies the sums paid.
A ruffian; a bravo; a destroyer.
A kind of soft yellow brick, easily cut, and used for facework.
A light sleigh drawn by one horse.
One who rides racehorses competitively.
That part of a variable resistor or potentiometer that rides over the resistance wire
An operator of some machinery or apparatus.
A dealer in horses; a horse trader.
A cheat; one given to sharp practice in trade.
A prostitute's client.
A rapist.
To ride (a horse) in a race.
To jostle by riding against.
definitions.example: They were jockeying for position toward the end of the race.

definitions.definition: To maneuver (something) by skill for one's advantage.
To cheat or trick.
More than half (50%) of some group.
definitions.example: The majority agreed that the new proposal was the best.

definitions.definition: The difference between the winning vote and the rest of the votes.
definitions.example: The winner with 53% had a 6% majority over the loser with 47%.

definitions.definition: Legal adulthood.
definitions.example: By the time I reached my majority, I had already been around the world twice.

definitions.definition: The office held by a member of the armed forces in the rank of major.
definitions.example: On receiving the news of his promotion, Charles Snodgrass said he was delighted to be entering his majority.

definitions.definition: Ancestors; ancestry.
(followed by a preposition) To advance very slowly, or by a small amount (in a particular direction).
definitions.example: Fearful of falling, he inched along the window ledge.

definitions.definition: To drive by inches, or small degrees.
To deal out by inches; to give sparingly.
(in combination) Being a certain number of inches in length.
The time of the day between dusk and night, when it gets dark.
The time of the day between the approximate time of midwinter dusk and midnight (compare afternoon); the period after the end of regular office working hours.
A concluding time period; a point in time near the end of something; the beginning of the end of something.
definitions.example: It was the evening of the Roman Empire.

definitions.definition: A party or gathering held in the evening.
A graphical representation of a particular emotion of the writer, used especially in SMS, email, or other electronic communication.
Moving or heading towards the east.
definitions.example: The eastbound train is late again!

definitions.definition: Toward the east.
definitions.example: He drove eastbound on the George Washington Bridge.

definitions.definition: An actual instance when a situation occurs; an event or happening.
(grammar) The lexical aspect (aktionsart) of verbs or predicates that change in or over time.

definitions.definition: A bodily response to early hypothermia.Wp

definitions.definition: To cause to shake or tremble, as a sail, by steering close to the wind.

definitions.definition: A multitude.
(of fish) To form into, or travel in a school.

The shooting forth of anything from a point or surface, like diverging rays of light.

definitions.definition: Firmness of conviction.

To persuade someone to do something which they are reluctant to do, especially by flattery or promises; to coax.
To raise using a jack.

definitions.definition: To raise or increase.

To steal something, typically an automobile. Shortened form of carjacking.

definitions.definition: To dance by moving the torso forward and backward in a rippling motion.
To hit (the ball) hard; especially, to hit (the ball) out of the field, producing a home run.
High on drugs or stimulants.
Broken, imperfect, especially when followed by 'up'. (See jacked up)

definitions.definition: Strong and/or muscled.
definitions.example: Wow, that guy is jacked!

definitions.definition: A final statement of terms or conditions made by one party to another, especially one that expresses a threat of reprisal or war.
To make mythical.
Of, relating to, operating by, or caused by magnetism.

definitions.definition: Having the properties of a magnet, especially the ability to draw or pull.
Determined by earth's magnetic fields.

definitions.definition: Having an extraordinary ability to attract.
definitions.example: He has a magnetic personality.

definitions.definition: Having, susceptible to, or induced by, animal magnetism.

definitions.definition: The smallest possible amount, quantity, or degree.
Characterised by the use of simple form or structures.
Characterised by the repetition and gradual alteration of short phrases.
The beliefs, ideas, or thoughts of mystics.
A doctrine of direct communication or spiritual intuition of divine truth.
A transcendental union of soul or mind with the divine reality or divinity.
Obscure thoughts and speculations.
That which impels or gives an impulse; an impelling agent.
One whose behaviour or personality is characterized by being impulsive.
Having the power of driving or impelling; giving an impulse; moving; impellent.
Actuated by impulse or by transient feelings; inclined to make rapid decisions without due consideration.
Acting momentarily, or by impulse; not continuous – said of forces.

A statement that, when taken in context, may actually mean something different from, or the opposite of, what is written literally; the use of words expressing something other than their literal intention, often in a humorous context.
Dramatic irony: a theatrical effect in which the meaning of a situation, or some incongruity in the plot, is understood by the audience, but not by the characters in the play.
Ignorance feigned for the purpose of confounding or provoking an antagonist; Socratic irony.
The state of two usually unrelated entities, parties, actions, etc. being related through a common connection in an uncommon way.
Contradiction between circumstances and expectations; condition contrary to what might be expected.
A person of low general intelligence.
A person who makes stupid decisions; a fool.
A person of the lowest intellectual standing, a person who lacks the capacity to develop beyond the mental age of a normal four-year-old; a person with an IQ below 30.
In an efficient or effective manner; with powerful effect.
Essentially, in effect, for all practical purposes.
The hair that grows over the bone ridge above the eye socket.
A dormer, usually of small size, whose roof line over the upright face is typically an arched curve, turning into a reverse curve to meet the horizontal line at either end.
A clump of waste fibres that builds up in a roller machine.
To build up waste fibres in a roller machine.
To signal with one's eyebrows.
To show mercy.
To keep.
To give up To deprive oneself of, as by being frugal; to do without; to dispense with; to give up; to part with.
The act by which something or someone is spared.
Prudent and restrained in the use of resources; careful, economical or frugal.
To pack again.
To clean the bearings and replace the grease on a wheel.
The realm of faerie; enchantment, illusion.

A mythical being with magical powers, known in many sizes and descriptions, although often depicted in modern illustrations only as a small sprite with gauze-like wings, and revered in some modern forms of paganism.
An enchantress, or creature of overpowering charm.
A male homosexual, especially one who is effeminate.
A member of two species of hummingbird in the genus Heliothryx.
Like a fairy; fanciful, whimsical, delicate.
The act of very quickly closing both eyes and opening them again.
The time needed to close and reopen one's eyes.
A text formatting feature that causes text to disappear and reappear as a form of visual emphasis.
A glimpse or glance.
Gleam; glimmer; sparkle
The dazzling whiteness about the horizon caused by the reflection of light from fields of ice at sea; iceblink
(in the plural) Boughs cast where deer are to pass, in order to turn or check them.
An ability that allows teleporting, mostly for short distances
To close and reopen both eyes quickly.

definitions.definition: To flash on and off at regular intervals.

definitions.definition: To perform the smallest action that could solicit a response.
To shut out of sight; to evade; to shirk.

To turn slightly sour, or blinky, as beer, milk, etc.

The wood or bark of this walnut tree.
The nut of this walnut tree.
A dye made from the fruit of this walnut tree.
The souari nut.

definitions.definition: The cashew tree.
A cashew nut.
The wood from the mahogany tree or other trees from the family Meliaceae.

Of good omen; indicating future success.
Conducive to success.

definitions.definition: Marked by success; prosperous.
To lay or place; to put (one thing to another)

To to address; to refer; generally used reflexively.
To submit oneself as a candidate (with the adposition "to" designating the recipient of the submission, and the adposition "for" designating the position).

definitions.definition: To pertain or be relevant to a specified individual or group.

definitions.definition: To busy; to keep at work; to ply.
To visit.
To grasp and pull with the fingers or fingernails.
